summary: Small game-mode matching PR touching gm_1601, gm_16AE, gm_16F1, and gmmain_lib. It tightened function signatures and headers, introduced a local getSpawnPoint inline to deduplicate repeated spawn-point Stage_80224E64 call patterns, rewrote a compound exclusion condition into explicit continue guards, initialized a rankings stack array directly instead of loading removed static data symbols, and changed gmMainLib_8015DADC to return bool. Bot report records +668 matched-code bytes, 2 new matches, 6 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched-item regression.

key_files.role: Adjusted argument types for fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C from s32-style parameters to u8 and removed an unnecessary cast at a callsite.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c hunks for gm_801674C4, fn_801695BC, fn_8016989C
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_8016989C as a new 100.00% match and fn_801695BC as a small regression

key_files.role: Updated public prototypes for fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C to match recovered u8 parameter types.
Matched and improved several game-mode routines by changing post-increment shape and extracting repeated spawn-point lookup code into static inline getSpawnPoint.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16AE.c added static inline getSpawnPoint and replaced repeated Stage_80224E64 blocks
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_8016DEEC as a new 100.00% match and improvements for fn_8016E2BC, gm_8016EDDC, fn_8016D634

key_files.role: Changed fn_8016FAD4 kind parameter type, zero-initialized rankings directly, removed static-data loads, and rewrote gm_80173EEC_inline filtering into explicit continue guards.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16F1.c fn_8016FAD4 signature and rankings initialization
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_8016FAD4 improvement and .sdata2 improvement

key_files.role: Updated fn_8016FAD4 prototype from u32 kind to int kind.
Removed three static data declarations lbl_804DA2E8, lbl_804DA2EC, and lbl_804DA2EE after rankings became a zero-initialized local array.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16F1.static.h removed static s32/s16/s8 declarations

key_files.role: Changed gmMainLib_8015DADC return type from s32 to bool while keeping bit-test return expression.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmmain_lib.c gmMainLib_8015DADC return type change

what_changed.text: Recovered narrower u8 parameter types for fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C and synchronized gm_1601.h prototypes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h signature changes

what_changed.text: Changed fn_8016D634 from pre-increment compare to post-increment compare on lbl_8046B6A0.unk_30.
Added static inline getSpawnPoint(int i, Vec3* v) and replaced four repeated spawn-point conditional blocks in gm_16AE.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16AE.c getSpawnPoint addition and replacements in fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016E2BC, gm_8016EDDC

what_changed.text: Changed fn_8016FAD4 kind parameter from u32 to int in source and header.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16F1.c and gm_16F1.h fn_8016FAD4 signature changes

what_changed.text: Replaced manual stack-array initialization from three static data symbols with u8 rankings[7] = { 0 } and removed the now-unused static declarations.
Rewrote gm_80173EEC_inline's combined exclusion condition into sequential early-continue checks before calling gmMainLib_8015DADC.
Changed gmMainLib_8015DADC return type from s32 to bool and updated its header declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmmain_lib.c and gmmain_lib.h gmMainLib_8015DADC changes

smart_moves.text: Kept recovered signatures truthful across C and header files instead of leaving mismatched prototypes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c/gm_1601.h and gm_16F1.c/gm_16F1.h matching signature edits

smart_moves.text: Factored a repeated spawn-point fallback idiom into a static inline helper, reducing duplicate generated-looking blocks while preserving call behavior in multiple local sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16AE.c getSpawnPoint addition and replacements

smart_moves.text: Converted a bit-test helper's return type to bool and used it with !gmMainLib_8015DADC(i), improving source-level expression of predicate semantics.
Removed local static data declarations when a zero-initialized local rankings array was sufficient for the changed source shape.

decomp_lessons.text: When a repeated local conditional dispatch pattern appears across nearby game-mode functions, a small static inline helper can be a matching-friendly way to express likely authored source while reducing duplicated decompiler-shaped blocks.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new match for fn_8016DEEC and improvements for fn_8016E2BC/gm_8016EDDC

decomp_lessons.text: Narrowing parameter and return types to match observed use can affect matching and should be propagated to headers in the same PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8 parameter changes for fn_801695BC/fn_8016989C
raw_diff: int kind change for fn_8016FAD4
raw_diff: bool return change for gmMainLib_8015DADC

decomp_lessons.text: A cleaner source form can still carry a localized regression; here fn_801695BC's signature change is associated with a -4 byte unmatched-item regression in the bot report, so postmortems should preserve both gains and losses.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801695BC signature changed from s32 parameters to u8 parameters
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_801695BC regression from 70.85% to 70.22%

naming_conventions.text: The new helper name getSpawnPoint is a direct role name for the local operation of resolving a fighter spawn_point fallback and writing a Vec3.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline void getSpawnPoint(int i, Vec3* v)

naming_conventions.text: No broad global naming convention is established by this PR; most address-style function and data names remain unchanged.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: functions such as fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016FAD4, gmMainLib_8015DADC remain address-style

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a static inline helper rather than a normal out-of-line function for repeated spawn-point logic, consistent with preserving callsite code shape for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16AE.c static inline getSpawnPoint and callsite replacements

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Changed increment timing from ++x compare to x++ compare in fn_8016D634, a small expression-shape tactic that improved the unmatched item according to bot output.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report fn_8016D634 improved from 98.04% to 98.91%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Replaced a dense combined condition with explicit continue guards for excluded IDs before a bool predicate call in gm_80173EEC_inline.
Used zero-initialized stack array syntax to avoid explicit static-data loads for rankings.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16F1.static.h removed three static data declarations
text_corpus: bot report .sdata2 improvement from 50.00% to 80.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Existing PAD_STACK calls remain in touched functions, but the PR evidence does not show new PAD_STACK additions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16AE.c and gm_16F1.c context contains PAD_STACK lines but no added PAD_STACK hunks

review_feedback.text: No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: only PR body and decomp-dev bot issue comment loaded

notes: The diff and bot report provide strong evidence for changed files, code edits, and reported matching outcomes.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments, so author intent and reviewer rationale are not available.
The bot report supports claimed new matches and improvements, but no local build, objdiff logs, or detailed per-function assembly evidence are included beyond the automated summary.
One regression is explicitly reported for fn_801695BC and should not be hidden by the overall positive match result.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Static inline helper for repeated spawn-point fallback in gm_16AE
accepted_candidate_records.text: In gm_16AE, repeated blocks that choose between player index and FighterMatchInfo[i].spawn_point before calling Stage_80224E64 were replaced with static inline getSpawnPoint(int i, Vec3* v). The bot report recorded fn_8016DEEC reaching 100% and improvements in related functions, making this a supported local example of helper extraction for repeated matching patterns.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16AE.c getSpawnPoint addition and replacements
text_corpus: bot report fn_8016DEEC new match and fn_8016E2BC/gm_8016EDDC improvements

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve regressions in match-ledger summaries
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2473 improved overall matched code and added two matches, but the bot report also listed fn_801695BC as a -4 byte regression after its parameter type change. Intake records should retain such local regressions for future cleanup searches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report regression row for fn_801695BC

source_update_candidates.title: Game-mode matching activity in src/melee/gm
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2473 is a merged game-mode matching PR that touched gm_1601, gm_16AE, gm_16F1, and gmmain_lib under src/melee/gm.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: all changed paths under src/melee/gm
source_update_candidates.reason: May help path-scoped retrieval for future gm matching PRs; should be curated as a path fact rather than accepted from intake alone.

source_update_candidates.title: No new global standard proposed; example supports existing helper/type/header standards
source_update_candidates.text: This PR supplies examples for existing standards on inferred authored source style and truthful headers, but the evidence is not broad enough to create a new global rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: Route as a curator note only; existing standards already cover the observed patterns.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all repeated conditionals should become static inline helpers; this PR only supports the local spawn-point pattern in gm_16AE.
Do not claim fn_801695BC matched or improved; the bot report explicitly lists it as a regression.
Do not treat removed lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2EC/lbl_804DA2EE as a general data-ownership rule without additional section/symbol evidence.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
